title: Advisory/scan phase contract exhaustion degrades to SKIPPED+WARN instead of cycle-fatal — formatting flakes must not kill shipped-quality work
summary: SUBSUMED 2026-07-14 by retro-bridge-timeout-width10 (now the class fix: any non-floor phase failure — bridge-launch exit code OR verdict-contract exhaustion — degrades to SKIPPED+WARN, never cycle-fatal). Kept as a verification companion: if the class fix lands first, this becomes a green regression check for the verdict-contract trigger specifically. Lowered to 0.8 to avoid a duplicate lane. ORIGINAL: Cycle 742 (2026-07-13): phase test-amplification emitted no parseable verdict ([bad_verdict] expected one of [PASS FAIL WARN SKIPPED]) after 2 corrections and the WHOLE CYCLE failed — an LLM output-formatting flake in an advisory-tier phase was cycle-fatal. The integrity floor treats build/audit/tdd as load-bearing (ship => build AND audit AND tdd-unless-trivial); the scan/amplification phases (test-amplification, secret-leak-scan, flake-rerun-scan, error-handling-scan) are NOT in the floor and already fail-open at the spine level (76 'spine not satisfied ... proceeding fail-open' WARNs in one batch). Make verdict-contract exhaustion on NON-floor phases consistent with that: after corrections are exhausted, record verdict SKIPPED with reason bad_verdict, WARN loudly, count it in the dossier, and continue the cycle. Keep cycle-fatal semantics for floor phases. Also raise correction attempts from 2 to 3 with a stricter format-only reprompt on the final attempt (cheap, catches most formatting flakes).
acceptance: dossier records skipped_phases with reasons; go test -race PASS; apicover clean
